title: rock-eval TeamCreate Structured Output Schemas
description: All structured output schemas used by rock-eval TeamCreate teammates. Design basis: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-06-17-rock-eval-teamcreate-design.md §6. Each schema is enforced via Agent tool with schema option; teammates MUST emit a matching object. Legacy v2 prompt-driven runs (references/team-orchestration.md) do NOT use these schemas — they rely on prompt-level 'iron rules'.

SmokeOutput.title: SmokeOutput
SmokeOutput.description: Output schema for OracleChecker (task-2) and NopChecker (task-3). One object per smoke run; both smoke teammates use the same shape.

experiment_id.description: The experiment_id returned by `regression.py run`. Required so the Lead can reference the smoke experiment for later diagnose/retry. NEVER let teammates infer 'latest file'; must be the concrete id returned by the run.

ok.description: true = smoke check passed. For OracleChecker pass means every task reward ≈ full score (scoring chain OK). For NopChecker pass means every task completed normally with reward ≈ 0 (environment/image/cluster OK). false = anomaly detected.

detail.description: Optional. REQUIRED when ok=false — one sentence describing the anomaly (e.g. 'task t-001 reward=0.0, expected ≈ 1.0' for OracleChecker, or 'task t-002 failed with RuntimeError: image pull timeout' for NopChecker). Keep it to a single concise sentence; raw logs/trajectory must NOT be pasted here. Ignored on success.

ConfigConfirmOutput.title: ConfigConfirmOutput
ConfigConfirmOutput.description: Output schema for task-1 (Lead confirms parameters with user and decides whether to smoke). Produced by the Lead itself (team leader) — not by a spawned teammate — so the Lead writes the result into the task's metadata when marking task-1 completed.

bench.description: Bench template name to run. Resolved live via `rc agent run --help`; never hardcoded.

dataset.description: Dataset name. May be null if the user only provided a bench and the teammate resolves the dataset from the bench template; otherwise must match `datasets.name` from `rc agent bench getconfig <BENCH> --raw`.

split.description: Dataset split identifier (e.g. 'delivery_0609-cn'). Null only when the bench does not expose a split.

agent.description: Real agent name for the full run (NOT oracle/nop). Resolved live via `rc agent run --help`.

smoke_tasks.description: The representative task ids used for the smoke check. Required when smoke=true. Pick 2-3 representative tasks spanning difficulty/file size when possible. Ignored when smoke=false.

window_size.description: Global concurrency cap for the full run. Recommended ~10 to respect shared ROCKCLI quota. Smoke runs override this to 2 internally.

pass_through.description: Pass-through parameters forwarded verbatim to `rc agent run`: image, cluster, model, namespace, cpus, memory, ee (env vars), set, config, pre/no-pre, async-mode, user-id, base-url, api-key, etc. Keys are the flag name without the leading `--`; values are the flag value as a string (use 'true' for boolean flags). Only include flags the user explicitly specified — never inject defaults.

smoke.description: Whether to run the Phase 1 smoke check (OracleChecker ∥ NopChecker). true by default; set to false ONLY when the user explicitly declines smoke (e.g. small quick verification).

alignment.description: Whether this run targets a published/known score to align against (Phase 1.5). When true, Lead will additionally create task-1.5 to confirm the baseline file.

notes.description: Optional free-form notes from the user conversation (e.g. 'only retry the java100 split', 'skip alignment').

SmokeDecisionOutput.title: SmokeDecisionOutput
SmokeDecisionOutput.description: Output schema for task-4 (Lead aggregates OracleChecker + NopChecker smoke results into a Phase 2 / Operator decision). Produced by the Lead. Replaces the old 'manual aggregation' soft rule with an explicit state-machine decision.

decision.description: The routing decision after Phase 1. 'proceed' = both smoke checks passed → unblock task-5 (full run) and task-6 (monitor). 'operator' = at least one smoke check failed but root cause looks like a tunable param (e.g. image pull timeout → larger memory/poll-timeout) → unblock task-11 directly to skip the full run and retry tuned. 'abort' = environment is fundamentally broken (e.g. cluster unreachable) → stop and report to user.

oracle_ok.description: Whether the OracleChecker (task-2) smoke check passed. Copied from SmokeOutput.ok of task-2.

nop_ok.description: Whether the NopChecker (task-3) smoke check passed. Copied from SmokeOutput.ok of task-3.

oracle_experiment_id.description: experiment_id of the OracleChecker smoke run (from task-2 SmokeOutput). For traceability and possible later diagnose.

nop_experiment_id.description: experiment_id of the NopChecker smoke run (from task-3 SmokeOutput).

reason.description: One sentence justifying the decision. e.g. 'oracle+nop both ok, proceed to full run' or 'nop image pull timeout, route to Operator for memory bump'.

tuning_hint.description: Optional. Required when decision='operator'. A concrete hint for the Operator (e.g. 'increase --memory to 8Gi and --poll-timeout to 900').

RunnerOutput.title: RunnerOutput
RunnerOutput.description: Output schema for task-5 (Runner executes the full run in background and reports by milestone). Runner stays alive across milestones; this object is the final aggregation written into task-5.metadata.output when Runner marks the task completed. Milestone notifications before the final aggregation are sent via SendMessage (free-form, schema not enforced) to keep Lead's context light.

experiment_id.description: The experiment_id returned by `regression.py run`. Written verbatim from the run command's actual return value — NEVER inferred from 'latest file'.

status.description: Terminal status of the full run. 'done' = all tasks finished (success or error). 'stuck' = milestone monitoring detected prolonged no-progress / timeout (Runner could not complete, see stuck_reason). 'interrupted' = Runner was externally stopped (e.g. user / Operator loop reset).

success.description: Count of tasks in success state at the moment Runner stopped polling.

error.description: Count of tasks in error state.

dispatched.description: Count of tasks still dispatched (not yet transitioned to success/error). Should be 0 when status='done'; >0 only for stuck/interrupted.

total.description: Total task count of this run (success + error + dispatched + any skipped). Used by downstream tasks to compute pass rate.

stuck_reason.description: Optional. Required when status='stuck'. One sentence describing where it got stuck (e.g. 'no progress for 3 consecutive milestones on task t-042'). Ignored for done/interrupted.

MonitorPatrolOutput.title: MonitorPatrolOutput
MonitorPatrolOutput.description: Output schema for task-6 (Monitor starts periodic patrol via CronCreate and aggregates patrol verdicts). Monitor runs each patrol as a Cron-fired sub-invocation; the final aggregation written into task-6.metadata.output is this object. Monitor also pushes one-line patrol verdicts to Lead via SendMessage during the run (schema not enforced, for light-weight status).

experiment_id.description: The experiment_id under patrol. MUST be the same as RunnerOutput.experiment_id (both reference the full run experiment).

verdict.description: Aggregated patrol verdict over the whole run. 'normal' = no patrol raised the suspicious criteria. 'suspicious' = at least one patrol fired the criteria (dispatched non-decrease streak ≥3 OR error piling up). When suspicious, sample_message carries the one-line rock-agent-debug confirmation.

patrol_count.description: Number of patrol cycles executed before Runner stopped or Monitor stopped the Cron. 0 means patrol never fired (e.g. run finished before first cron tick).

final_dispatched.description: Last snapshot's dispatched count (from final report --format json).

no_decrease_streak.description: Final value of the dispatched non-decrease streak counter (criterion ①). ≥3 means criterion fired at least once.

sample_message.description: Optional. Required when verdict='suspicious'. One-line confirmation from rock-agent-debug deep-dive (e.g. 'job t-042 container exited 0 but no result.json — fake execution'). Ignored when verdict='normal'.

ReportOutput.title: ReportOutput
ReportOutput.description: Output schema for task-7 (Monitor generates the final HTML report after the full run settles, sync included). Produced by Monitor when it observes RunnerOutput.status=='done' (or Lead instructs finalization on stuck/interrupted).

experiment_id.description: The experiment_id of the full run this report summarizes.

dispatched.description: Tasks still dispatched at report time. Should be 0 for a clean done run; >0 indicates the report was finalized on a stuck/interrupted run.

html_path.description: Absolute path to the generated HTML report file (from `report <EXP_ID> --format html`). NEVER paste HTML content — only the path.

exception_groups.description: Top exception groups sorted by count descending. Used by Lead to dispatch Diagnostician(s). Each entry corresponds to one potential diagnose target.

type.description: Exception type label (e.g. 'RuntimeError', 'TimeoutError', 'ImagePullBackoff'). From the report's grouping.

sample_message.description: One short sample message from this group (one line, ≤200 chars). Diagnostician later pulls full detail via diagnose --remote/--trajectory.

DiagnoseOutput.title: DiagnoseOutput
DiagnoseOutput.description: Output schema for task-8 / task-9 (Diagnostician deep-dives one exception group). One object per diagnosed group. Diagnostician keeps raw trajectory/remote logs in its own context and emits ONLY this conclusion object — pasting raw logs here is a violation.

root_cause.description: One sentence root cause conclusion. e.g. 'OOM killed at 4Gi memory limit during long-context inference'. If evidence insufficient, set to '证据不足，建议挖 <another task id>'.

exception_type.description: The exception type of the diagnosed group (matches ReportOutput.exception_groups[].type).

tasks.description: Task ids covered by this diagnosis (the representative task(s) Diagnostician actually ran diagnose on, plus other tasks inferred to share the same root cause).

is_param_issue.description: Whether the root cause is fixable by tuning run params (memory/cpus/poll-timeout/image/cluster/model/agent/namespace/ee). true → routes to Operator via task-10. false → not a param issue (may be a bench/agent bug, dataset problem, or genuinely inconclusive).

suggestion.description: Concrete suggestion for the Operator. When is_param_issue=true, this MUST name the specific param(s) to change and the suggested value (e.g. 'increase --memory to 8Gi'). When is_param_issue=false, give a non-param suggestion (e.g. 'report to bench owner — likely a task definition bug', or 'evidence insufficient, retry diagnose on t-099').

TuningDecisionOutput.title: TuningDecisionOutput
TuningDecisionOutput.description: Output schema for task-10 (Lead aggregates Diagnostician conclusions and decides whether to tune). Produced by Lead. Also encodes the loop-termination policy: when no_improvement_count ≥3 OR all is_param_issue=false, should_tune=false and the pipeline terminates.

should_tune.description: Whether to dispatch the Operator (task-11). true = at least one DiagnoseOutput.is_param_issue=true AND loop_count<3 AND risk tolerable. false = terminate the loop (either no param issue found, or 3 consecutive tuning attempts without improvement, or user-declined high-risk).

risk_level.description: Risk level of the proposed tuning. 'none' only when should_tune=false. 'low' = only memory/cpus/poll-timeout/ee (Operator self-serve). 'high' = touches image/cluster/model/agent/namespace (needs Lead → user confirmation before Operator acts).

tuning_params.description: Optional. Required when should_tune=true. The params to change, keyed by flag name (no leading --), value as string. Same shape as ConfigConfirmOutput.pass_through. Only includes the deltas — Operator merges with the previous run's pass_through.

reason.description: One sentence justifying the decision. e.g. 'Diagnostician A found OOM, bump memory; low risk' or '3 consecutive tuning attempts no improvement, stop'.

loop_count.description: Number of tuning loops executed so far (this is loop_count + 1's decision). Lead reads previous task-10 metadata.loop_count and increments. Terminates at 3 per design §5.2.

OperatorOutput.title: OperatorOutput
OperatorOutput.description: Output schema for task-11 (Operator executes stop → destroy → tune → ready-to-rerun). Produced by Operator. Does NOT itself start the new run — that's task-5's job in the loop reset.

action_taken.description: What the Operator did. 'executed_low_risk' = low-risk tuning applied (memory/cpus/poll-timeout/ee), old tasks stopped, sandboxes destroyed, new config prepared. 'pending_high_risk_confirmation' = high-risk tuning proposed, awaiting Lead → user confirmation (action_taken carries the proposed plan only). 'skipped_no_action' = no tuning needed (Lead's should_tune=false edge case).

new_experiment_id.description: Optional. The new experiment_id prepared for the re-run (if Operator pre-created a placeholder experiment or the re-run command was issued with --prepare-only). Usually null — the actual new experiment_id comes from Runner's re-run in the next task-5 cycle. Keep this field for traceability when Operator does pre-create.

new_pass_through.description: Optional. The merged pass-through params for the next run (previous pass_through + tuning_params from task-10). Lead reads this to render the new Runner prompt for the looped task-5. Null when action_taken != executed_low_risk.

detail.description: One sentence summary. e.g. 'memory 4Gi→8Gi applied, 2 stale sandboxes destroyed, ready to re-run' or 'proposed switching image to rock:2.1, awaiting user confirmation'.
